label: Advanced Buildings
title: Advanced BuildingEntity API
lead: Advanced building prefab hooks, worldgen details, loot crate patterns, and minigame-driven buildings.
bodyHtml:
When to use this page

Use this page when your building needs prefab hooks, custom components, or special interaction logic. If the object is basically a prop or a vein, the normal BuildingEntity page is probably enough.

Advanced building fields

These are the knobs you reach for when a building needs extra Unity behavior, custom runtime wiring, or minigame-style interaction. Normal props usually do not need any of these.

Field Type What CUCoreLib uses it for
ConfigurePrefabAction<GameObject>Runs once while the prefab is being built.
ConfigureInstanceAction<GameObject>Runs on each spawned instance after instantiation.
ComponentsType[]Extra Unity components added to the prefab before it is cached.
SpawnComponentsList<string>Qualified MonoBehaviour type names added to each spawned building instance the first time it wakes up.

Spawn-time MonoBehaviours

Use SpawnComponents when you want a quick per-instance script hook without manually wiring ConfigureInstance or building a larger prefab setup path.

BuildingEntityRegistry.Register("campheater", new CustomBuildingEntityDefinition
{
    Name = "Camp heater",
    Description = "A small heater with a simple runtime script.",
    Sprite = mySprite,
    Placement = BuildingPlacementType.Floor,
    SpawnComponents = new List<string>
    {
        "MyMod.CampHeaterRuntime, MyModAssembly"
    }
});

Keypad and lockpick minigames

Vanilla buildings use an Openable component for these interactions. If isKeypad is true, Openable.OnUse() launches KeypadMinigame. Otherwise it launches LockpingMinigame with lockpickAnglePrecision scaled by the run setting lockpickprecision.

instantOpen bypasses the minigame and just zeroes the building's health. That is the simplest pattern if you want a locked or coded building that still fits the normal game flow.

public sealed class SafeDoorScript : MonoBehaviour
{
    private void Awake()
    {
        Openable openable = gameObject.AddComponent<Openable>();
        openable.isKeypad = true;
        openable.lockpickAnglePrecision = 1.25f;
    }
}

For a keypad-style building, give the prefab an Openable with isKeypad = true. For a lockpick-style building, leave that false and adjust lockpickAnglePrecision to tune how strict the minigame is.
